meta.manuscript_location: Shrine of the Book, Israel Museum, Jerusalem

preamble.summary: Isaiah 41 contains God's challenge to the nations and the assurance to Israel as His servant. The chapter has 29 verses preserved in columns XXXIII-XXXIV of 1QIsaiah-a. Variants are mostly orthographic with a few moderate readings.
preamble.notable_variants: Verse 7: the scroll has a slightly different word order in describing the craftsman. Verse 14: the scroll reads 'men of Israel' (metei yisrael) where the MT has the unusual 'men of Israel' (metei — sometimes read as 'dead ones of' by some). Verse 27: a minor plus in the scroll.
preamble.scroll_condition: Well preserved throughout. No significant damage or lacunae.

The consonantal text is identical but 1QIsaiah-a's orthography suggests a slightly different vocalization. The description of the idol-maker fastening the image with nails is substantively the same in both texts.

1QIsaiah-a uses plene spelling with vav in 'elohekha' and a different suffix form. Same meaning.

The MT's metei is an unusual form that could theoretically be parsed as 'dead ones of Israel,' though all ancient translations understood it as 'men of.' 1QIsaiah-a's spelling confirms the reading 'men of Israel,' removing any ambiguity.

verses.variant_rendering: First to Zion — behold, behold them!
verses.mt_rendering: First to Zion: Behold, here they are!
variant_notes: 1QIsaiah-a appears to have a slightly expanded reading with a doubled hinneh ('behold, behold') where the MT has a single hinneh hinnam. The duplication may reflect emphasis in the scroll tradition or a scribal expansion for rhetorical effect.
No significant variant. The scroll reads identically to the MT here.
